description: Open-ended inventive problems outside any published benchmark. Used to demonstrate what the TRIZ Engine plugin does on real creative tasks versus vanilla Claude. No ground truth — qualitative showcase only.

problems.title: Tangle-free wired earbuds
problems.problem_statement: Wired earbuds tangle in pockets and bags. Design a solution that eliminates the tangling entirely (not reduces it) without: (a) asking the user to change behavior, (b) adding a case, clip, or storage accessory, (c) going wireless, (d) adding motors or battery-powered active components. The solution must preserve current audio quality, weight, and price point. Identify the core contradiction, pick the most relevant TRIZ inventive principles, and propose a concrete engineering solution that eliminates the contradiction at the source.

problems.title: Shower that halves water use with identical experience
problems.problem_statement: Design a shower that uses 50% less water per minute while keeping the subjective user experience (warmth, rinse feel, spray coverage, hair-wash performance) indistinguishable from a standard 9 L/min shower. Constraints: no heated recirculation loops requiring long warm-up, no user interaction beyond the normal handle, no mist-only experience (users must feel real droplets), must work with standard household 2-3 bar water pressure. Identify the core contradiction, map to TRIZ parameters, and propose a concrete mechanism that eliminates — not merely trades off — the contradiction.
